udt_struct_v0.doc: One action bundled into a pool `submit` call.
udt_struct_v0.name: Request

fields.doc: Asset address relevant to the request.
fields.name: address

udt_struct_v0.doc:
Snapshot of one reserve as returned by `get_reserve`.

Field names and types must EXACTLY match blend-contracts-v2 pool wasm @ ba22b487.
The `asset` and `scalar` fields are part of the real struct and must be present
for correct XDR decoding (Soroban decodes maps by matching ALL fields in the struct).
udt_struct_v0.name: Reserve

fields.doc: Reserve runtime data (accrues on interest updates).
fields.name: data

fields.doc: SCALAR_7 constant embedded in the reserve snapshot (10_000_000).
fields.name: scalar

udt_struct_v0.doc:
Flash-loan parameters.

Field names and order MUST match the pool wasm exactly (XDR struct encoding is by field name).
Source: blend-contracts-v2 pool wasm @ ba22b487, confirmed via `stellar contract inspect`:
\xe2\x80\xa2 amount: I128
\xe2\x80\xa2 asset: Address
\xe2\x80\xa2 contract: Address
udt_struct_v0.name: FlashLoan

fields.doc: Amount of `asset` to flash (in underlying stroops).
fields.name: amount

fields.doc: Receiver contract address (the flash-loan callback target; \xe2\x89\xa0 `from`).
fields.name: contract

udt_struct_v0.doc:
A user's position snapshot keyed by reserve index (u32).

Field names must match blend-contracts-v2 pool wasm (pool/src/storage.rs Positions).
Maps are reserve-index \xe2\x86\x92 share amount (b-token or d-token shares, SCALAR_12).
udt_struct_v0.name: Positions

udt_struct_v0.doc:
Reserve runtime data (updated on every interest accrual).

Field names and types must EXACTLY match blend-contracts-v2 pool wasm @ ba22b487.
Source: pool/src/storage.rs `ReserveData`.
Rates are SCALAR_12 (1_000_000_000_000).
Available underlying \xe2\x89\x88 (b_supply \xc3\x97 b_rate \xe2\x88\x92 d_supply \xc3\x97 d_rate) / SCALAR_12.
udt_struct_v0.name: ReserveData

fields.doc: b-token rate: underlying per b-token share (SCALAR_12).
fields.name: b_rate

fields.doc: Backstop credit accumulated for this reserve.
fields.name: backstop_credit

fields.doc: d-token rate: underlying per d-token share (SCALAR_12).
fields.name: d_rate

udt_struct_v0.doc:
Reserve configuration set by the pool admin (persistent; changes rarely).

ALL fields from the real pool wasm MUST be present and correctly named \xe2\x80\x94
Soroban decodes structs by unpacking the full field set; a missing or extra
field panics with `ScErrorCode::UnexpectedSize`.  This struct mirrors
blend-contracts-v2 `ReserveConfig` field-for-field.
udt_struct_v0.name: ReserveConfig

fields.doc: Decimals for the asset (not used in exec_op, but present in the real struct).
fields.name: decimals

function_v0.doc:
Blend flash-loan callback (ModERC-3156 interface).

Called by the source pool with `exec_op(from, token, amount, fee=0)`.
Public by ABI necessity; all legitimate paths reach it only via
`migrate \xe2\x86\x92 source_pool.flash_loan`.  Guarded by `consume_staged_params`
which enforces: NoStagedParams, source_pool auth, SamePoolNotAllowed,
AssetMismatch, AmountMismatch (see consume_staged_params doc above).

Cross-pool supply/borrow (slice 3), swap (slice 4), and walking skeleton
(slice 5) follow after the guard layer.
function_v0.name: exec_op

function_v0.doc:
Stage params on `receiver` and call `source_pool.flash_loan` atomically.

Call stack (no re-entry):
migrator.migrate \xe2\x86\x92 receiver.stage_params (done) \xe2\x86\x92 source_pool.flash_loan \xe2\x86\x92 receiver.exec_op
function_v0.name: migrate

function_v0.doc:
Stage migration params for the calling position owner.

Public entry point called by the `RefinanceMigrator` contract BEFORE `flash_loan`.
Separated from the flash_loan invocation to avoid Soroban re-entry:
- Soroban prohibits a contract from having two frames on the call stack simultaneously.
- `migrate` on the receiver would be: receiver.migrate \xe2\x86\x92 pool.flash_loan \xe2\x86\x92 receiver.exec_op
(receiver appears twice \xe2\x86\x92 re-entry error from the Soroban host).
- Fix: a separate `RefinanceMigrator` contract calls `receiver.stage_params` (frame A,
completes), then calls `pool.flash_loan` (frame B \xe2\x86\x92 receiver.exec_op frame C).
Frames A and C are sequential, not concurrent \xe2\x86\x92 no re-entry.

Requires auth from `route.from` (position owner signing the migration).
Anyone may call this, but the stored params are useless without source_pool calling exec_op.
function_v0.name: stage_params

udt_struct_v0.doc:
One hop in an Aquarius `swap_chained` call.

Encodes the real Aquarius router's per-hop tuple `(Vec<Address>, BytesN<32>, Address)`:
- `tokens`:    ordered token pair `[token_a, token_b]` (must be sorted per the router).
- `pool`:      pool index hash (identifies the specific pool for this token pair).
- `token_out`: which token in `tokens` to receive from this hop.

Two-hop example (PYUSD\xe2\x86\x92USDC\xe2\x86\x92EURC):
```text
hop1 = SwapHop { tokens: [PYUSD, USDC], pool: <pyusd_usdc_hash>, token_out: USDC }
hop2 = SwapHop { tokens: [USDC, EURC],  pool: <usdc_eurc_hash>,  token_out: EURC }
```
udt_struct_v0.name: SwapHop

fields.doc: Pool index hash (Aquarius identifies pools by `(tokens_salt, pool_index)` \xe2\x80\x94 this is the\npool-index portion, a `BytesN<32>` that uniquely selects a pool within a token pair).
fields.name: pool

fields.doc: Token to receive from this hop.
fields.name: token_out

fields.doc: Sorted token pair for the pool lookup.
fields.name: tokens

udt_struct_v0.doc: A Blend pool address. Distinct from `AssetAddr` to prevent pool/asset transposition.
udt_struct_v0.name: PoolAddr

udt_struct_v0.doc: New-debt (PYUSD) amount in stroops.\nDistinct from `CollateralAmt` and `OldDebtAmt`.
udt_struct_v0.name: NewDebtAmt

udt_struct_v0.doc: Old-debt (EURC) amount in stroops.\nDistinct from `CollateralAmt` and `NewDebtAmt`.
udt_struct_v0.name: OldDebtAmt

udt_struct_v0.doc: The staged payload bundling both structs \xe2\x80\x94 what `stage` writes and `take` removes.
udt_struct_v0.name: StagedParams

udt_struct_v0.doc: Collateral (USDC) amount in stroops (7-decimal fixed-point).\nDistinct from `NewDebtAmt` and `OldDebtAmt`; passing one where the other is expected\nis a compile-time type error.
udt_struct_v0.name: CollateralAmt

udt_struct_v0.doc:
Stable topology chosen once per migration strategy and reused across invocations.

These fields are determined by the migration strategy (which pools, which assets, which
router) and do not change between parameter computations for the same route.
udt_struct_v0.name: MigrationRoute

fields.doc: Collateral asset relocated between pools (USDC); also the flash asset.
fields.name: collateral

fields.doc: Pool that receives collateral and issues new debt (YieldBlox V2).
fields.name: dest_pool

fields.doc: Position owner whose debt is being refinanced.
fields.name: from

fields.doc: Pool that issues the flash loan and holds the old debt (Fixed V2).
fields.name: source_pool

fields.doc: Ordered list of swap hops: first hop's token_in = new_debt; last hop's token_out = old_debt.\nTwo-hop example: [SwapHop{[PYUSD,USDC], pool_hash_1, USDC}, SwapHop{[USDC,EURC], pool_hash_2, EURC}].
fields.name: swap_chain

udt_struct_v0.doc:
Ephemeral amounts freshly computed seconds before submission and fixed at signing time.

These expire with the ledger: staleness is bounded by re-reading live reserve state
immediately before construction and pinning all amounts here so they match signed auth entries.
udt_struct_v0.name: MigrationAmounts

fields.doc: N \xe2\x80\x94 PYUSD to borrow on dest pool. Fixed at sign time; never re-capped in `exec_op`.
fields.name: borrow_amount

fields.doc: C \xe2\x80\x94 USDC collateral to relocate (= flash amount).
fields.name: collateral_amt

fields.doc: Conservative floor on `dest_pool.reserve(collateral).c_factor` in SCALAR_7.\nGuard rejects a reserve whose CF has degraded below this threshold.
fields.name: min_cf

fields.doc: R_static \xe2\x80\x94 minimum EURC out from the swap; also the settlement Repay amount.
fields.name: min_old_debt_out

fields.doc:
\xce\xb5 \xe2\x80\x94 d-token share-alignment overage (capped); sizes the flash to a share boundary so
`from`'s flash liability clears to exactly 0. C/d_rate-dependent. Funded by the
receiver, not `from`. Caveat: assumes d_rate stable between off-chain read and
on-chain exec (holds within one tx). See \xc2\xa73.4 / interfaces.md \xc2\xa75.
fields.name: share_overage

fields.doc: Unix timestamp deadline for the swap. Reserved for future use; the Aquarius router\ndoes not take a deadline parameter \xe2\x80\x94 this field is retained in MigrationAmounts for\nABI stability and potential future use with a deadline-aware router variant.
fields.name: swap_deadline

cases.doc: Guard: `exec_op` called with no staged params for `from`.
cases.name: NoStagedParams

cases.doc: Business: aggregator delivered zero or negative tokens (actual \xe2\x89\xa4 0).
cases.name: SwapUnderMin

cases.doc: Business: `borrow_amount` is zero or dest pool has insufficient liquidity.
cases.name: BorrowUnavailable

cases.doc: Guard: c_factor(collateral) < amounts.min_cf (admin-reduced reserve guard).\nCollateral sufficiency vs. new_debt is enforced by the dest pool's HF\xe2\x89\xa51 invariant on submit.
cases.name: InsufficientCollateral

cases.doc: Guard: staged params are internally inconsistent \xe2\x80\x94 `eps < 0` or\n`c_supply \xe2\x89\xa4 0` (i.e. `share_overage \xe2\x89\xa5 collateral_amt`).
cases.name: MalformedParams
